meaning: n.厌恶人类的人,不愿与人来往者
examples: Consider both an avid cocktail party hostess with hundreds of acquaintances and a grumpy misanthrope, who may have one or two friends?
To Robert, a misanthrope who'd barely mastered E-mail in his earlier life, this networked world is a multitasking hell.
He even had a brief career in law, which may partly explain his inclusion of lawsuits and legal strife in The Misanthrope.
One myth is that he was a misanthrope, a grumbling recluse, whose work was as morose as his life.
Michel Houellebecq, a 40-year-old ex-Marxist, was hailed in some quarters as a scourge of the 1968ers and damned in others as an anti-liberal misanthrope.
